subtasks.description: Evaluate the hotfix deployment request against change management and maintenance window policies. Determine if engineer u_7732 has authorization for out-of-window production deployments to prod-us-east-1, and assess compliance with maintenance windows and emergency deployment procedures.

plan.cost_rationale: Single policy_action subtask at mid tier. This requires evaluation of multiple policy dimensions: (1) engineer authorization level, (2) maintenance window compliance rules, (3) production environment restrictions, and (4) emergency deployment exception criteria. Mid tier can handle multi-signal policy reasoning. No additional subtasks needed—the query does not ask for historical audit trails, cross-source reconciliation, or tool orchestration; it is a governance decision gate."

output.reason: Deployment denied on multiple grounds: (1) POL-401: User u_7732 holds role 'engineer', which does not meet the minimum required role of 'senior-engineer' for production deployments. (2) POL-402: The current time (Tuesday 02:15 UTC) is outside the approved maintenance window, and no CAB approval has been provided or referenced in this request. (3) POL-403: The approved maintenance window is Saturday 10 PM – Sunday 6 AM UTC; Tuesday 02:15 UTC falls entirely outside this window. All three applicable policies are violated. The hotfix deployment cannot proceed without (a) escalation to a senior-engineer or higher to sponsor/execute the deployment, and (b) obtaining emergency CAB approval for an out-of-window production change.
applied_constraints: User role 'engineer' is insufficient; minimum required role is 'senior-engineer' per POL-401.
Current time Tuesday 02:15 UTC is outside the approved maintenance window (Saturday 22:00 – Sunday 06:00 UTC) per POL-403.
CAB approval is mandatory for any out-of-window production deployment per POL-402; no approval on record.
Engineer u_7732 must escalate to a senior-engineer or engage the CAB emergency change process before this deployment can be authorized.
